description: Figure 5 presents X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns of Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.8C low-density steel subjected to warm multiaxial forging (MAF) for 0, 1, 3, and 5 passes. The bottom trace (MAF-0) exhibits sharp γ-austenite peaks indexed as γ(111), γ(200), γ(220), γ(311), and γ(222), along with a κ(220) reflection from κ-carbides. MAF-1 shows additional δ(211) and κ(222) peaks with reduced overall intensity. With increasing MAF passes (MAF-1 to MAF-5), the diffraction peaks progressively broaden and diminish in intensity, indicating increased dislocation density and grain refinement. The patterns confirm, as stated in the caption and text, that carbide phases remain essentially unchanged during MAF, allowing precipitation strengthening to be treated as constant. The peak broadening data were used to quantify dislocation density, which together with EBSD-measured grain sizes, was incorporated into a modified Johnson-Cook constitutive model to predict strain-dependent yield strength evolution.
